# color-mix()

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/color_value/color-mix" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=color-mix%28%29" status="Snapshot pre-CR snapshot, publishable" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Mix colors in a chosen color space.

## Representative syntax

```css
color-mix(in oklch, var(--brand) 70%, white)
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="color-mix()" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="The important part is the relationship between colors: one semantic color generates a maintainable set of states." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use color-mix() in theme and state systems when you need to build color variations from semantic inputs instead of hand-tuning every state. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>A warning banner can derive its background, border, and text color from one token and still adapt in dark mode.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Perceptual spaces help with ramps, but they do not replace contrast checks against real text sizes and backgrounds.</p>
</section>
</div>
